title: Wintertodt
wikitext:
{{Dangerous}} {{HasStrategy}} {{Infobox Scenery |version1 = Howling |version2 = Quiescent |name1 = Howling Snow Storm |name2 = Quiescent Snow Storm |image1 = [[File:Howling Snow Storm.gif|300px]] |image2 = [[File:Quiescent Snow Storm.gif|300px]] |release = [[8 September]] [[2016]] |update = The Wintertodt |members = Yes |quest = No |location = Prison north of the [[Wintertodt Camp]] |options1 = None |options2 = None |examine1 = The vortex spins furiously as the cold pierces through your bones. |examine2 = The vortex is less furious. |map = {{Map|name=Wintertodt|zoom=1|x=1630|y=3981}} |id1 = 29308 |id2 = 29309 |leagueRegion = Kourend }} The '''Wintertodt''' ({{IPAc-en|ˈ|w|ɪ|n|t|ɚ|t|ɒ|d}} {{Respell|win|ter|tod}}{{CiteVideo|author=Old School RuneScape|title=Wintertodt BTS - Old School RuneScape's New Skilling Boss|url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=e38SSdRsF5A&t=7s|timestamp=00:06|date=7 September 2016|site=YouTube}}) is a minigame-style boss that is fought using skills rather than combat. It is located in the [[Northern Tundras]] in [[Great Kourend]], bringing a storm of perpetual winter and unfathomable cold. According to the [[look-out]], it is unclear what the Wintertodt actually is, but its ability to alter the weather to extremely low temperatures has earned it notoriety among the people of [[Great Kourend]]. Players must assist the [[Order of the Sacred Flame]], a special group of [[Arceuus]] mages, to keep the Wintertodt at bay. As servants of the sacred flame, only they know the spells required to subdue the Wintertodt, so they are heavily relied upon in the defence of Great Kourend. While the [[pyromancer]]s' magical abilities are extraordinary, their skills in other areas are limited and thus require assistance from skilled adventurers. To help subdue the Wintertodt, players are required to have at least level 50 in [[Firemaking]]. Higher [[Woodcutting]] level increases the success rate of chopping [[bruma roots]]. Skill levels in [[Herblore]], [[Fletching]], and [[Construction]] do not affect the speed of their related activities at all. However, having higher levels always grants more experience (see [[Wintertodt#Reward experience|reward experience]] section below or [[Wintertodt/Strategies#Experience|full experience rates]] on the strategies page). The Wintertodt is considered to be a boss, not a minigame, and '''dying here is unsafe.''' The normal rules for loss of items apply, and Hardcore Ironmen will lose their Hardcore status if they die here. Dying, logging out, or leaving the prison before the end of a round will result in losing '''all''' points for the current round. Leaving the prison will also delete any items specific to the boss such as [[rejuvenation potions]] and [[bruma kindling]]. [[Ironman_Mode|Ironmen]] are eligible for rewards from Wintertodt when defeating it alongside other players. Subduing the Wintertodt while earning at least 500 points is a requirement for the [[medium Kourend & Kebos Diary]]. {{ToC}}{{Clear}} ==History== [[File:Doors of Dinh.png|thumb|The [[Doors of Dinh]] continue to seal the Winter Beast to this day.]] While it is not clear what the Wintertodt is, there are multiple theories by various people in the [[Wintertodt Camp]]. [[Ignisia]] believes it to be an evil spirit,{{CiteNPC|npc=Captain Kalt|quote=Ignisia claims it's some kind of evil spirit.}} [[Ish the Navigator]] believes it felt the power of the [[Dark Altar]] and attacked Great Kourend to seek it,{{CiteNPC|npc=Ish the Navigator|quote=[The Wintertodt] felt the [Dark] Altar's power and wanted to consume it for itself.}} while [[Undor]] believes it to be a powerful fire spirit.{{CiteNPC|npc=Undor|quote=They say it's some kind of powerful fire spirit.}} During the 2nd decade (''1,400 years ago''), the Wintertodt (known back then as the Winter Beast) ravaged the lands north of Kourend, causing them to experience perpetual winter, with livestock and crops freezing. It continued to ravage the [[Northern Tundras]] for another three decades, with Great Kourend's attempts to subdue it unsuccessful. On 1,778 - 5 (''1,360 years ago''), fearing the Winter Beast might eventually ravage the kingdom, Lord Arceuus I ordered the construction of the [[Arceuus Library]],{{CiteNPC|npc=Official Order of Construction|quote=It is with this in mind that I, Lord Arceuus, Ruler of Great Kourend, hereby order the construction of a library. A library filled with texts, documents and research from all of Great Kourend. The library shall be constructed here, within Arceuus, and enchanted with spells from our greatest mages to protect the contents from evil.}} filled with texts, documents, and research from all cities, to preserve the history of Great Kourend. Meanwhile, the most important texts were stored in the [[Library Historical Archive]], a repurposed chamber of the [[Tower of Magic]], for further study and additional protection. On 1,748 - 7 (''1,340 years ago''), a master Lovakengj smith named [[Dinh]] formulated a plan to defeat the Winter Beast. Meeting with the [[Order of the Sacred Flame]], Dinh believed that the roots of the Bruma tree, a magical tree lying within the heart of the [[Northern Tundras]], could emit the heat required to subdue the Winter Beast. Dinh proposed to construct a large prison in which to lure the Beast in and seal it with a large set of doors made from various materials, including [[lovakite]] and bark from the Bruma tree, with enchantments placed by the Order.{{CiteNPC|npc=Winter's End|quote=Dinh has constructed the doors to the prison from various metal compounds including Lovakite but they must be reinforced with Bruma bark and enchanted by the Order.}} With Lord Lovakengj II approving of the plans, the prison's construction was completed 800 days later, with known casualties of sixteen Order members and two scouts. On 2,548 - 7, after five decades, the Winter Beast was successfully imprisoned and sealed behind the [[Doors of Dinh]],{{CiteNPC|npc=Winter's End|quote=Rejoice! Our mission has succeeded, the Beast has been contained! The Bruma tree burns bright in the night sky, warming the northern tundra and holding back the Beast.}} with Lord Lovakengj II declaring the day as Winter's End. Over a thousand years later, the Doors have begun to fail due to being damaged by someone or something, creating a small hole in the doors. Due to this, the Wintertodt has been drawing a small amount of power into the prison.{{CiteNPC|npc=Undor|quote=It's not much, but it's enough for that Wintertodt to be able to draw some power into the prison. Probably why them fiery lot have had so much trouble recently.}} This hole is fixed by Undor during [[The Forsaken Tower]] [[quest]] after being given [[Dinh's hammer]]. Despite this, the doors were already damaged for such a time that the Wintertodt has already gained a lot of power,{{CiteNPC|npc=Undor|quote=The doors were damaged for a while and the Wintertodt will have gained a lot of power.}} and Undor believes that it will be many years before things return to normal.{{CiteNPC|npc=Undor|quote=Even though the doors are fixed now, it will take many years before things get back to normal.}} It is later revealed in [[A Kingdom Divided]] that the doors were sabotaged by one of the Pyromancers sent to subdue the winter beast, with collusion from [[Xamphur]], Xeric's closest ally. It is not known what interested Xamphur, but during the quest, the [[Tasakaal]] create a [[shielding potion]] for the adventurer to apply to the doors to prevent further damage. ==Transportation== {{Map|align=right|width=250|height=400|zoom=1|x=1640|y=3911 |mtype:line,stroke-width:5 ,1630:3965,1630:3937,1627:3933,1627:3930,1630:3927,1637:3926,1640:3924,1644:3920,1644:3917,1642:3914,1640:3913 ,1635:3912,1611:3912,1609:3911,1607:3908,1607:3891,1608:3888,1609:3886,1615:3880,1630:3876 ,1633:3874,1634:3872,1634:3840,1636:3837,1639:3836,1666:3836 |mtype:dot,fill:#00FF00,1630:3940 |mtype:dot,fill:#FFA500,1671:3833 |mtype:dot,fill:#03d196,1630:3840 |mtype:rstext,weight:bold,label:Games necklace,colour:#00FF00,1632:3947 |mtype:rstext,weight:bold,fontsize:12px,label:Fairy ring
{{Fairycode|cis}},colour:#F0FF00,1640:3870 |mtype:rstext,weight:bold,fontsize:12px,label:Lovakengj
Minecart
Network,colour:#FFA500,1671:3850 |mtype:rstext,weight:bold,fontsize:12px,label:Arceuus
Library
Teleport,colour:#03d196,1610:3838 }} There are four main methods for travelling to the Wintertodt camp in order to enter the [[Doors of Dinh]]. These options, ordered from fast to slow, are: * Players can reach the camp via [[games necklace]], provided they have already taken [[Veos]]' ship to Great Kourend once. * Players can choose the [[Northern Tundras]] option in the [[Lovakengj Minecart Network]] which brings them just south-east of Wintertodt. * Players can use the [[fairy ring]] code {{Fairycode|CIS}} and walk west along the path, provided they have visited Great Kourend previously through some other means. * Players can use the [[Arceuus Home Teleport]] by switching to the [[Arceuus spellbook]] by speaking with [[Tyss]]. This can be done with no requirements and is particularly useful for players who chose to start their Ironman accounts by training at Wintertodt, as there are few fast travel methods at low levels for iron accounts. === Bank chest === There is a tent with a [[bank chest]] just east of the game necklace teleport location. Players can use this bank to prepare for the fight, or as a close bank to a teleport for tasks such as [[Slayer]] and [[Farming runs]]. Note that players fighting the Wintertodt will need to '''leave the game''' to use the bank. ==Official worlds== The following [[Server#Themed servers/worlds|official worlds]] are assigned to Wintertodt: {| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: left;" !World !Location !Members !Activity {{WorldLine|307|United States (west)|mems=yes|Wintertodt}} {{WorldLine|309|United Kingdom|mems=yes|Wintertodt}} {{WorldLine|311|Germany|mems=yes|Wintertodt}} {{WorldLine|389|Australia|mems=yes|Wintertodt}} {{WorldLine|402|United States (east)|mems=yes|Wintertodt}} |} ==Requirements== *Level {{SCP|Firemaking|50}} is required to participate in the Wintertodt fight. *Completion of {{SCP|Quest}} [[Druidic Ritual]] is optional to manually create [[rejuvenation potion]]s; otherwise, players can have [[Brew'ma]] do so on their behalf. * Owning a [[player-owned house]] is required to gain [[Construction]] experience from repairing broken [[brazier]]s. * Items (most are obtainable from [[crate (wintertodt)|crates]] upon entering the [[Doors of Dinh]]): ** An [[axe]] ([[bronze axe]] obtainable in the Wintertodt) ** A [[knife]] (obtainable in the Wintertodt) ** A [[hammer]] (obtainable in the Wintertodt) ** A [[tinderbox]] (obtainable in the Wintertodt) or [[bruma torch]] ** Some [[Rejuvenation potion (unf)|rejuvenation potions]] to heal ([[Rejuvenation potion (unf)|unfinished potions]] and [[bruma herb]]s obtainable in the Wintertodt; optional if paired with [[Hitpoints#Regeneration|Hitpoints regenerating items]] such as [[Hitpoints cape]] and [[regen bracelet]]) ** At least four pieces of [[Wintertodt/Warm clothing|warm clothing]] (optional, highly recommended) === Warm clothing === {{Main|Wintertodt/Warm clothing}}Throughout the fight, the player will take intermittent passive damage as the cold of the Wintertodt begins to seep into their bones. This passive damage is scaled to each player's [[Firemaking]] level as well as the number of braziers that are currently lit. Wearing [[Wintertodt/Warm clothing|warm or winter-themed equipment]] will reduce the damage taken. '''Four''' of these warm items need to be equipped to receive the maximum damage-reduction. If the player is not wearing a minimum of four pieces, upon entering the prison, a game message will appear stating "''You feel the cold wind strike you as you enter; perhaps you should find some warmer clothes.''" ==Fighting the Wintertodt== [[File:Fighting the Wintertodt.png|thumb|right|Players fighting the Wintertodt.]] {{Main|Wintertodt/Strategies}} The Wintertodt cannot be attacked by conventional [[combat]]. Instead, players must help the [[pyromancer]]s drain its energy by lighting and maintaining the [[brazier]]s around the prison. To do this, players must chop the roots of the bruma tree found near the braziers to obtain [[bruma root]]s, optionally fletch them with a [[knife]] into more potent [[bruma kindling]], then add them to the burning braziers. === Warmth Meter === The warmth meter, visible on the HUD at the top left of the player's game client, acts as the player's health when inside the prison. '''Dropping to 0% warmth will cause instant [[death]].''' Players can restore their warmth by eating any type of food that heals at least 4 Hitpoints (such as a bite of cake), which will restore 35% warmth, or by drinking a dose of [[rejuvenation potion]], which will restore 30% warmth. Foods which heal in two parts such as [[hunter meat]] will restore warmth twice for a total of 70%. Players will also naturally regenerate 8% warmth per minute, with an additional 1% regenerated for each piece of [[warm clothing]] worn (up to 4). Various health regeneration methods, such as [[Hitpoints#Regeneration|Hitpoints regenerating items]], [[Rapid Heal]], and [[Heal Other]], will further aid warmth regeneration. A [[ring of life]] will activate if warmth drops below 10%. A [[Phoenix necklace|Phoenix Necklace]] will also activate at 20% or less and restore Warmth. Rejuvenation potions can easily be made inside the prison, and they are also used to heal the [[Pyromancer|pyromancers]] whenever they are damaged or incapacitated. If [[Druidic Ritual]] has not been completed, [[Brew'ma]] can combine the ingredients on the player's behalf. ===Damaging the Wintertodt=== The Wintertodt will be weakened by the power of the bruma flames that are channeled by the pyromancers. Thus it is important to keep the [[brazier]]s lit and the pyromancers alive in order to defeat it. The braziers can be lit with a tinderbox or bruma torch and repaired with a hammer. The flames can be fueled by adding [[bruma root]]s or [[bruma kindling]], though this does '''not''' cause the fight to progress any faster. Each pyromancer only has 14 health and will be incapacitated after taking two hits from the Wintertodt. If a pyromancer falls to 0 health, she cannot continue draining the Wintertodt's energy, nor can her brazier be relit, until a player heals her. Pyromancers can be fully healed via their "Help" option, requiring one dose of rejuvenation potion. ===Wintertodt's attacks=== [[File:Wintertodt Safe Area.png|thumb|The area safe from the Wintertodt's attacks.]] {{Main|Wintertodt/Strategies#Wintertodt's attacks}} The Wintertodt uses five kinds of attacks during the fight: *Standard attack - All individual players will take periodic damage as the cold of the Wintertodt begins to seep into their bones. The damage taken is based on the player's total Firemaking level, and is drastically reduced while wearing warm clothing. *Area attack - Heavy snow will occasionally appear over a 3x3 area centred on a specific player as a warning, then fall to the ground causing damage to all players currently in the 3x3 area. *Brazier heavy attack - Heavy snow will occasionally appear over a brazier as a warning, then the brazier will shatter. Players standing directly next to a brazier when it shatters will take damage. *Brazier light attack - Light snow will occasionally appear over a brazier as a warning, then it will extinguish. This does not cause damage. *Pyromancer attack - Light snow will occasionally appear over an individual pyromancer as a warning, then she will take damage. This is not dangerous to other players. The amount of time between these attacks is random{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ed|url=https://twitter.com/JagexEd/status/869286904730181632|date=29 May 2017|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/27963835|archivedate=11 December 2023|quote=The amount of time between each attack is random.}} but the frequency significantly depends on the Wintertodt's remaining energy. Standard attacks will be more common at first; later in the fight, brazier and pyromancer attacks will be more common. ===HUD=== [[File:Wintertodt boss HUD.png|thumb|right|The HUD for the boss.]] Upon entering the Wintertodt area, a HUD will appear in the top left of the screen. There are several icons on the HUD: *Unlit fire - The brazier has not yet been lit. *Lit fire - The brazier has been lit. *Crafting icon - The brazier has been damaged and needs to be repaired. *Red wizard hat - The pyromancer at that brazier has been incapacitated and needs to be healed. ===Reward points=== Players earn points based on the activities they perform during the battle. The following activities provide points: {| class="wikitable" !Activity !Skills involved !Points |- |Lighting a brazier |{{SCP|Firemaking}} |25 |- |Adding Bruma roots to a brazier |{{SCP|Woodcutting}}/{{SCP|Firemaking}} |10 |- |Adding Bruma kindling to a brazier |{{SCP|Woodcutting}}/{{SCP|Fletching}}/{{SCP|Firemaking}} |25 |- |Repairing a destroyed brazier |{{SCP|Construction}} |25 |- |Healing a pyromancer |{{SCP|Herblore}} |75 |} The rewards for defeating the Wintertodt are based on the player's points acquired throughout the fight. Players must collect a minimum of 500 points to be rewarded anything for their efforts. The reward will consist of the ability to search the [[Reward Cart]] (see [[#Rewards|rewards]] section below) and [[Firemaking]] experience. If the player does not achieve 500 points, a chat message will state "''You did not earn enough points to be worthy of a gift from the citizens of Kourend this time.''" There is no current known limit to the number of rewards points that can be earned in a single fight. ===Reward experience=== Players earn experience based on the activities they perform during the battle. The following activities provide experience as a multiple of the player's current level (for example, with level 50 Firemaking, lighting a brazier with a multiplier of 6x will reward 300 Firemaking experience): {| class="wikitable" !Activity !Skill rewarded !Level multiplier |- |Lighting a brazier |{{SCP|Firemaking}} |6x |- |Cutting a bruma root |{{SCP|Woodcutting}} |0.3x |- |Feeding a bruma root |{{SCP|Firemaking}} |3x |- |Fletching a bruma root |{{SCP|Fletching}} |0.6x |- |Feeding a bruma kindling |{{SCP|Firemaking}} |3.8x |- |Fixing a broken brazier |{{SCP|Construction}} |4x |- |Creating a rejuvenation potion |{{SCP|Herblore}} |0.1x |- |Subduing the Wintertodt with 500+ points |{{SCP|Firemaking}} |100x |} ==Rewards== {{Main|Reward Cart}} Succeeding in subduing the Wintertodt with a minimum of 500 points will reward players with [[Firemaking]] [[experience]] (''Firemaking level * 100''), as well as being owed at least two rewards from the [[reward cart]]. Every 500 additional points will guarantee an extra reward owed. Additional points over each 500 point threshold give a linearly scaled percentage chance for an extra reward. For example, 1200 points would guarantee 3 rewards plus a 200/500 (40%) chance of getting a fourth reward. The reward cart can hold a maximum of 8,000 rewards. The player's skill levels will determine the rewards the player gets from searching the cart. High levelled players will get rarer rewards, with lower levelled rewards becoming less common the higher the player's level in that skill is. At the highest point, low levelled rewards are removed from the drop table. While players with low levels in those skills can get rare rewards, the drop rate is very low. The herb, ore, fish, uncut gem, seed drops, and logs are based on the player's [[Herblore]], [[Mining]], [[Fishing]], [[Crafting]], [[Farming]], and [[Woodcutting]] levels respectively,{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1051830039346597888|date=15 October 2018|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/100059|archivedate=30 May 2020}} at the time of ''searching'' the cart. {{Wintertodt/Rewards}} ==Collection log== There are a total of 10 items to collect from the [[Wintertodt]]. {| class="wikitable lighttable individual" style="text-align:left;" ! colspan="3" | Items |- |{{plink|Phoenix}} |{{plink|Tome of fire (empty)}} |{{plink|Burnt page}} |- |{{plink|Pyromancer garb}} |{{plink|Pyromancer hood}} |{{plink|Pyromancer robe}} |- |{{plink|Pyromancer boots}} |{{plink|Warm gloves}} |{{plink|Bruma torch}} |- |{{plink|Dragon axe}} |{{NA}} |{{NA}} |} ==Combat Achievements== {{Sync}} {{Combat Achievements list|Wintertodt|mobname=the Wintertodt}} ==Music== {| class="wikitable sortable" |- !Name ! class="unsortable" | Unlock details ! class="unsortable" | Music track |- |[[The Doors of Dinh]] |Unlocked in the Wintertodt Camp. |[[File:The Doors of Dinh.ogg]] |- |[[Ice and Fire]] |Unlocked in Wintertodt's prison. |[[File:Ice and Fire.ogg]] |} ==Gallery== RuneFest- Old School Reveals (7).jpg|Official artwork depicting the Wintertodt, revealed during RuneFest 2015. Wintertodt - early development vs final product.jpg|An image of the Wintertodt when development began in comparison to the final product. Wintertodt prison.png|The prison containing the Wintertodt. Wintertodt face.gif|Slowing down the storm animation reveals a "face" resembling an {{WP|oni}} face (👹). ==Changes== {{Subject changes header}} {{Subject changes |date = 23 April 2025 |update = Poll 84: Misc Mayhem |change = * The warmth meter has been made narrower. * The Hitpoints bar is no longer shown when being hit by the cold. }} {{Subject changes |date = 8 January 2025 |update = Run Energy Changes |change = References to a "Reward Pile" have been removed when looting from the reward cart. }} {{Subject changes |date = 16 October 2024 |update = Halloween 2024 |change = * The number of points awarded for healing [[pyromancers]] has been increased from 30 to 75. * Cold hitsplats now appear darker on other players to more easily identify when you have been hit. }} {{Subject changes |date = 9 October 2024 |update = Wintertodt Updates & Varlamore Drop Rates |poll = Poll 82 - Minigame Changes |change = * A warmth meter has replaced [[Hitpoints]] as the resource required to maintain meaning that Wintertodt's basic and special attacks now damage to the warmth rather than Hitpoints. * Two new sets of [[sprouting roots]] have been added to each side of the lobby. * Picking [[bruma herb]]s in Wintertodt no longer grants [[Farming]] [[experience]]. * A 'Drink' option has been added to the [[rejuvenation potion]] which will restore 30% of a player's Warmth per dose, totaling 120% for the full potion. * To prevent accidental potion-consumption, a left-click 'Help' option has been added to the [[pyromancer]]s. * Various additional sources of regeneration have been added into the warmth system, such as [[Heal Other]], [[Redemption]], the [[regen bracelet]], and the [[Hitpoints cape]]. * The following items now count as [[warm clothing]]: ** [[Wolf mask]] ** [[Earmuffs]] ** [[Cap and goggles]] ** [[Woolly hat]] ** [[Bobble hat]] * The cooldown for the interruption that occurs by the Wintertodt's basic attacks while fletching bruma roots into [[bruma kindling]] has been increased. * [[Supply crate (discontinued)|Supply crates]] have been replaced with a reward cart. Every previous crate and its number of searches will now be a search in the reward pile, similar to [[Tempoross]]. * The number of rewards claimed from Wintertodt can now be tracked directly in the [[collection log]]. * When entering Wintertodt, there is now a warm clothing indicator message that will appear in chat indicating if a player is below the four-piece warm clothing cap. }} {{Subject changes |date = 13 September 2023 |update = The Path of Glouphrie |poll = Awakened DT2 Bosses & Poll 80 |change = The Wintertodt no longer has a randomised respawn timer, and now always respawns in one minute. }} {{Subject changes |date = 22 March 2023 |update = Quest Speedrunning's Final Batch |change = Damage in Wintertodt will now visibly interrupt [[Fletching]] animations. }} {{Subject changes |date = 3 March 2021 |update = TzHaar-Ket-Rak's Challenges |change = All max capes now count as warm items at the Wintertodt, like the Firemaking skillcape does. Previously only the infernal max cape and fire max cape worked this way! }} {{Subject changes |date = 28 March 2019 |update = The Seed Vault |change = The minimum respawn time of the Wintertodt has increased to 45 seconds. }} {{Subject changes |date = 6 October 2016 |update = The Hill Giant Boss |poll = |change = * Rewards from supply crates are now dependent on levels in various skills: High Farming level yields better seed drops, higher Crafting level yields better gem drops, higher Mining yields better ores, and Higher Fishing yields better fish. * Full sets of the Pyromancer outfit can be exchanged with Ignisia for a supply crate which provides four loot rolls. }} {{Subject changes footer}} ==Developers== * '''Main developer(s)''': [[Mod Ed]], [[Mod Maz]]{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1291485590572212225|date=6 August 2020|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/101022|archivedate=10 August 2020|quote=[Hey, who did the work on Wintertodt?] Mod Ed, possibly with assistance from Mod Maz. GFX probably by Mod West. 'Ice and Fire' song by Mod Ian.}} * '''Art''': [[Mod West]] * '''Audio''': [[Mod Ian]] ==Trivia== *Upon release, the Wintertodt's table was much more profitable and was changed several days after release. The Wintertodt's drop table was soon nerfed yet again due to player feedback on its still-generous reward table, primarily in [[seeds]] such as [[ranarr]] (the increased supply of which resulted in their price crashing). The new drops became based on the player's levels in the skills the items were related to. *When you examine the Doors of Dinh, it will say "Hold the door!". This is a reference to S06E05 "The Door" from the HBO show ''{{wp|Game of Thrones}}''. *The "dying" animation of Pyromancers next to braziers is identical to that of Lunar Spell [[Dream]]. *On 25 October 2024 Mod Kirby referenced a "Red Herring"{{PlainCiteNews|url=https://secure.runescape.com/m=news/gielinor-gazette---october-2024?oldschool=true|title=Gielinor Gazette - October 2024|newsdate=25 October 2024}} recently added to the Wintertodt during the recent activity update on 9 October 2024. What this references is currently unknown. *It is not possible to cast [[High Level Alchemy]] inside the Wintertodt area while it is active. Attempting to do so produces the message, "That magic would interfere with the pyromancer's and is not permitted here." Perhaps this is intended to prevent players from reaching the minimum point threshold and then ignoring the boss instead of contributing. *After the release of the warmth meter on 9 October 2024, players who had entered the Wintertodt prior to that date will receive the following warning message upon entering: "Please be aware that the Wintertodt has been modified since your last encounter; for full details, please visit our website for the news article dated October 2024, or consult the Old School Runescape Wiki." ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Wintertodt}} {{Bosses}}
